data.content: Only one explicit `static` keyword found (the `Main` method in Program.cs). Let me look at the actual ReportGenerator to find hidden static dependencies accessed through lambdas/LINQ:

Here are all the **hidden static dependencies** inside `ReportGenerator.cs`, buried within lambdas and LINQ chains:

| Line | Static Call | Testability Problem |
|------|-------------|---------------------|
| 8  | `File.Exists(path)` | Filesystem access |
| 9  | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Non-deterministic time |
| 9  | `File.ReadAllText(path)` | Filesystem access |
| 17 | `File.WriteAllText(...)` | Filesystem side-effect |
| 17 | `DateTime.Now` | Non-deterministic time |
| 18 | `Console.WriteLine(...)` | Console I/O |
| 18 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Non-deterministic time |
| 19 | `Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable(...)` | Global state mutation |
| 19 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Non-deterministic time |
| 27 | `Directory.GetFiles(...)` | Filesystem access |
| 28 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Non-deterministic time |
| 29 | `File.ReadAllText(f)` | Filesystem access |
| 30 | `Environment.MachineName` | Machine-specific global |

**Summary:** The class has zero injected dependencies. All I/O and time are accessed via statics hidden inside lambdas, making it effectively untestable without the real filesystem and clock. To fix this you'd inject abstractions like `IFileSystem`, `ITimeProvider` (or .NET 8's `TimeProvider`), and `IConsole`.
